Hotkey Engine — keyboard shortcuts

Headless shortcut engine: it registers chords + sequences, listens for keydown, matches against active scopes with a typing-guard, and fires handlers. This page is a reference consumer — it renders the palette, modal, cheatsheet and the pending-sequence indicator from the engine's state.

The engine ships NO UI and NO CSS. Everything visible here is consumer-rendered. The engine only attaches one keydown listener and fires handlers / exposes getBindings() + state.

Live state — what the engine exposes

From getState() + subscribe() / on('sequence'). The pending box shows an in-progress sequence (press g and pause).

engine enabled platform – · mod = – scopes global pending · 0 bindings
Try a shortcut after disabling / unbinding — nothing fires.

1 · Chords

Modifier combos matched on the exact modifier set.

⌘Kopen the command palette (an overlay this page renders)
⌘Ssave — preventDefault stops the browser's Save dialog
?toggle the keyboard cheatsheet (a Shift+/ symbol binding)

Press ⌘S — you get a "Saved" flash and no browser Save dialog.

2 · Sequences (Vim-style)

Press the keys in succession. g alone is held until the timeout, since it is also the prefix of the longer sequences.

g ggo to top (flash)
g ihighlight the Inbox panel

Inbox panel — idle. Watch the pending box above light up g… as you press g.

3 · Scopes — a modal pushes a scope

Opening the dialog pushScope('modal'). While open, Esc closes it (shadowing the global Esc) and ⌘↵ confirms — a shortcut that only works in the modal scope.

Global Esc clears the nav selection (section 4); the modal's Esc shadows it.

4 · Typing-guard

j / k move the cursor below. Focus the input and they stop firing (you can type "jk" freely) — but ⌘S still saves.

  • Welcome aboardunread
  • Your weekly digestunread
  • Invoice #2043paid
  • Re: launch plan3 replies
  • Security alertimportant

Cursor at item 1.

5 · Shortcuts cheatsheet — rendered from getBindings()

This whole table is built from the engine's registered bindings (grouped by scope). It re-renders on every change — bind/unbind/scope. Press ? for the overlay version.

Activity — handlers the engine fired

Press a shortcut…

Command palette scope: palette · Esc to close

  • New document⌘N
  • Go to inboxg i
  • Toggle theme⌘⇧L

Typing here is guarded — bare shortcuts won't fire while this input has focus.

Settings scope: modal · Esc closes · ⌘↵ confirms

This dialog pushed the modal scope. Esc here closes the dialog (it shadows the global Esc); ⌘↵ confirms.

Keyboard shortcuts ? or Esc to close
